front: Film: five-part cycle named for a muscle in the testes; films represent male gonads in increasingly descended states
Film: third entry in this cycle was partially filmed at the Solomon R. Guggenheim Museum
Film: Richard Serra throws molten Vaseline at the Guggenheim ramp in a film from this series
Film: prologue and epilogue of the third entry are set at Fingal's Cave on Staffa
Film: the Loughton Candidate, a suit-wearing, red-haired faun, tunnels beneath the Isle of Man during a motorcycle race in the fourth entry
Film: double amputee athlete Aimee Mullins plays a woman cutting potatoes with knives on her feet and a crystal-legged 'Entered Novitiate' in 'The Order'
Film: one entry opens with images of a jeweled saddle, Bonneville flats, bees, and a logo
Film: three muscular faeries wearing yellow hoop skirts lie on a yellow picnic blanket near a cliff in an entry of this series
Film: pigeons tied to the genitals of a diva played by the artist in the fifth entry of this cycle
Film: five-part cycle that depicts a grotesque faun lowered onto a football field via balloons
Film: Norman Mailer played Harry Houdini in the second film of this cycle
Film: experimental filmed opera depicting Norman Mailer's reincarnation as various cars
Film: multimedia project series where the artist painted while jumping on a trampoline or while attached to bungee cords
Film: ninth entry features the artist and Bjork as 'occidental guests' on a Japanese harpoon ship who engage in Shinto rituals and turn into whales
Film: massive trilogy of linked installations, videos, art books, and films focused on NFL player Jim Otto
Artist: ex-partner of Bjork; contemporary artist known for multimedia projects including a five-part film cycle
Artist: collaborated with Elizabeth Peyton on a performance piece titled Blood of Two, involving a dogfish shark carcass
